title: galactia dimorpha
intro: Galactia dimorpha, also known as the American milkpea or American milkpea vine, is a perennial plant native to North America. It is a member of the pea family and is known for its clusters of small, fragrant flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce pain and swelling, and combat infections. Herbal preparations of Galactia dimorpha can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, cough, and skin conditions.
